commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
From. Hebrew, "without the blood of soldiers, without the fat of the valiant, the bow of Jonathan had never returned. "(Kennicott)
Fat. The entrails. It might also denote the most valiant of the soldiers, as we read of "the fat or marrow of corn "for the best, Psalm lxxx. 17. (Calmet)
Jonathan attacked the most courageous, and laid them dead at his feet. (Haydock)
Empty. Saul carried destruction wherever he went. Et nos tela, pater, ferrumque haud debile dextra, Spargimusnostro sequitur de vulnere sanguis. (Virgil, Æneid xii. 50.)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
